_meta.note: gpt-5.6 additional_tools harness preamble at input[0] (exec/wait/collaboration namespace). Trimmed from a real desktop capture; nested schemas kept byte-sensitive (grammar, encrypted flags, reserved collaboration.*).
_meta.purpose: contract fixture for additional_tools carriage round-trip (NOT a Copilot-behavior oracle)

format.definition:
start: pragma_source | plain_source
pragma_source: PRAGMA_LINE NEWLINE SOURCE
plain_source: SOURCE

PRAGMA_LINE: /[ \t]*\/\/ @exec:[^\r\n]*/
NEWLINE: /\r?\n/
SOURCE: /[\s\S]+/

cell_id.description: Identifier of the running exec cell.

max_tokens.description: Output token budget for this wait call. Defaults to 10000 tokens.

terminate.description: True stops the running exec cell; false or omitted waits for output.

yield_time_ms.description: Wait before yielding more output. Defaults to 10000 ms.

tools.name: send_message
tools.description: Send a message to an existing agent. The message will be delivered promptly. Does not trigger a new turn.

message.description: Message text to queue on the target agent.

target.description: Relative or canonical task name to message (from spawn_agent).

tools.name: spawn_agent
tools.description:
Spawns an agent to work on the specified task. If your current task is `/root/task1` and you spawn_agent with task_name "task_3" the agent will have canonical task name `/root/task1/task_3`.
You are then able to refer to this agent as `task_3` or `/root/task1/task_3` interchangeably. However an agent `/root/task2/task_3` would only be able to communicate with this agent via its canonical name `/root/task1/task_3`.
The spawned agent will have the same tools as you and the ability to spawn its own subagents.

Only call this tool for a concrete, bounded subtask that can run independently alongside useful local work; otherwise continue locally.
It will be able to send you and other running agents messages, and its final answer will be provided to you when it finishes.
The new agent's canonical task name will be provided to it along with the message.

Note that passing `fork_turns="none"` will not pass any surrounding context to the spawned subagent, which may cause the agent to lack the context it needs to complete its task, whereas `fork_turns="all"` will provide the subagent with all surrounding context.

fork_turns.description: Optional number of turns to fork. Defaults to `all`. Use `none`, `all`, or a positive integer string such as `3` to fork only the most recent turns.

message.description: Initial plain-text task for the new agent.

task_name.description: Task name for the new agent. Use lowercase letters, digits, and underscores.
